title: orchis latifolia subsp. lusitanica
intro: Orchis latifolia subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Portuguese orchid, is a subspecies of the Orchid family, specifically the Orchis latifolia plant, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
